sections.title: N+1 query patterns — N/A
sections.content: No database or API calls exist anywhere in the project (confirmed via read of `app.js`/`index.html`). The only persistence is `localStorage.getItem/setItem` on `STORAGE_KEY`.

sections.title: Unnecessary re-renders (React equivalent: full DOM rebuilds)
Caching opportunities — redundant array traversals
Memory leaks
sections.content: No real leak: `innerHTML = ""` discards old nodes and their listeners are garbage-collected with them (nothing external retains references), and the single `window.addEventListener("storage", ...)` (`app.js:239`) is registered once, not per render. The closer issue is **GC churn**: every render allocates 3 new DOM nodes + 3 new closures *per item*, even for items that didn't change — wasteful but not leaking.

sections.title: Redundant computations — forced reflow on unchanged items
sections.content:
`createTodoItem` (`app.js:177`) schedules an entrance animation for **every** item on **every** render:

```js
requestAnimationFrame(() => item.classList.add("visible"));
```

Since the whole list is rebuilt on any single change, toggling one checkbox replays the `transform`/`opacity` transition (`styles.css:105,126`) for *every* task in the list, not just the one that changed — unnecessary layout/paint work that scales with list size. Fix: only trigger the entrance animation for genuinely new todos, e.g. track previously-rendered ids in a `Set` and create the node already `.visible` for ids already in that set.

**Bottom line**: at the app's current scale (a personal to-do list with likely tens of items) none of this is causing visible jank — the architecture (full rebuild + per-item listeners) is simple and correct. The fixes above matter if the list is expected to grow into the hundreds; otherwise the event-delegation change is the one I'd make regardless, since it's a small, free cleanup.
